original_abstract: Program evaluation serves as a systematic process for gathering critical data on organizational implementation and decision-making frameworks. This study utilizes a comprehensive literature review of academic journals and e-books to provide a roadmap for educators tasked with assessing institutional initiatives. Our findings highlight that a rigorous analysis of evaluation outcomes is essential for determining the reach and efficacy of school programs from previous years. Furthermore, by situating these evaluations within the context of current educational policies, this research offers a strategic benchmark for quality assurance. Ultimately, these results provide a foundational framework for both administrators and supervisors to analyze past performance and dictate the trajectory of future school improvements.
humanized_abstract: Program evaluation is a process for gathering information about the implementation of programs and the process by which decisions are made. As a tool for the educator involved in the assessment of programs implemented in the schools, this study utilized a literature review of current journal and e-book literature to identify major dimensions that should be considered. Major findings include the importance of thorough examination of the impact of the evaluations that have been conducted of the programs of previous years, and that the evaluations should be carried out within the framework of current educational policy so that a standard of quality may be established. This study provides a base from which school administrators, and those in a supervisory capacity, may have a basis for assessing school activities and for planning improvements in the schools for years to come.
